home.delete.title: Delete project
home.delete.message: Delete project '{project_id}'? The entire project folder and all files inside will be removed.
home.new_project.title: New project
app.title: unidoc2md
models.title: Models
models.intro: On this screen you can enable and disable individual LLM provider models, and set their capabilities and pricing.
models.sync.message: Loaded data for {providers_count} providers, new models: {models_new}
models.detail.article:
Adjust registry fields for the selected model: enable or disable it, set the context window and per-million token prices, and mark input/output modalities and extra features (chat, tools, structured output, reasoning).

Click Save to apply changes. Invalid numbers show a validation error.
models.detail.empty_banner: Select a model on the left to view and edit its settings.
project.discovery.article:
Place files to process in the project documents folder; open it with the Open documents button.

After adding files, click Refresh — the table lists files in supported formats.

Search scope: Root only — files directly in the docs folder; Nested folders — recursive subfolders. Markdown output follows the same folder layout.

Supported formats:
- documents: pdf, docx, odt
- text: txt, md
- images: png, jpg, jpeg, webp, bmp, gif, tif, tiff, svg

there is currently no support for: legacy Word (.doc); spreadsheets and tabular files (xls, csv, and similar); presentations (pptx and similar). RTF and other formats are also unsupported.
project.save_modal.title: Save
project.save_modal.validation_message: Fix the errors and save again.
project.run_modal.title: Run
project_execution.folder_modal.title: Folder
project_execution.folder_hint.docs_missi: The «docs» folder was not found. Add a docs directory to the project root.
project_execution.folder_hint.result_mis: The «result» folder was not found. It appears after the save-results step.
project_execution.run_modal.title: Run
project_execution.tagging.skip_mode: Skip mode: LLM is not called.
project_execution.tagging.no_llm_config: Tags were not created: set a provider and model in project settings.
project_execution.tagging.create_tags_of: «Tag documents» is off: LLM is not called.
project_extract.article:
The «Data extraction» section controls how document content is obtained.

A key difficulty with many formats is that it is unclear what is on images: it may be a text scan, a photo, or a diagram.

Most settings relate to image handling; if you prefer not to tune everything, the defaults work for many cases.

Things to watch:
- Render scale and image size strongly affect cost and recognition time; values much above ~1500 px are rarely worth it
- If your PDFs are scans, they often have both an image layer and a low-quality OCR text layer; adaptive mode or full-page render is usually better
- If office documents truly have no images, disable image handling to save a bit on processing
project_extract.provider.markdown_extrac: Save as is — no changes. Save without YAML — strip front matter. Rebuild via LLM — prepare text for later generation (YAML removed).
project_extract.provider.pdf_extract_pro: «Adaptive» renders pages to images when large images or poor OCR text are detected. «Render all pages» sends every page as images
Page render scale for text recognition; higher values improve quality but increase processing cost
project_extract.provider.office_extract_: Works directly with document text; behavior for images depends on the mode. «Adaptive» skips tiny images such as logos
What to do with embedded images. If you are sure there are no scans or screenshots, choose «Ignore»
Images larger than this are scaled down proportionally. Larger images improve recognition quality but cost more
project_extract.provider.text_extract_pr: Simple loader: when enabled, text files are passed to the LLM unless extraction is turned off
project_extract.provider.image_extract_p: When image models are configured, the app tries to read text from images. Vector images (SVG) are rasterized using the size limit below
Images larger than this are scaled down proportionally. Larger images improve recognition quality but cost more
project_image_processing.logic_desc.skip: Images are not processed and are not copied to the results folder
project_image_processing.logic_desc.ocr_: OCR models are tuned to find text in images and usually give the highest accuracy. Best when you work with many document scans
project_image_processing.logic_desc.visi: Vision (multimodal) models are versatile for text recognition but may misread some parts
project_image_processing.yandex_key_bann: Yandex OCR is unavailable: the yandex API key is not set
project_image_processing.ocr.intro: Choose the OCR provider and model. For now selection is limited to Yandex services
project_image_processing.ocr.hint_model: Model to use; keeping «page» is recommended
project_image_processing.vision.intro: Configure available models from the main screen via «Model settings». Only models with image input support are listed here
project_image_processing.vision_model.re: If the model supports reasoning, enabling it can improve text recognition but increases API cost
project_image_processing.vision_model.te: Creativity (0.0 = none, 2.0 = max). 0.3 is a good default to fix minor OCR errors. Not all models support this parameter; if an error occurs, set the value to 1.0.
project_image_processing.vision_model.ex: Additional system-prompt instructions for image text recognition; optional
project_image_processing.article:
The «Image processing» section configures how text is recognized in images.

Available modes and models depend on API keys (openai, claude, etc.). Add keys from the main screen via «Settings».

Modes:
- Skip — images are not processed or saved.
- Yandex OCR — OCR-focused models; requires a configured Yandex API key.
- Vision LLM — multimodal models that can «see» images; requires a provider API key.

*Configure vision models from «Model settings» on the main screen: enable the model and check «Image input».
project_markdown.mode_desc.llm_processin: Text is sent to an LLM to format as Markdown (model settings below).
project_markdown.hint.provider: LLM provider for Markdown text processing
project_markdown.hint.temperature: Response randomness (0.0 = deterministic). Not all models support this parameter; if an error occurs, set the value to 1.0.
project_markdown.hint.system_prompt: Extra system instructions for layout; optional
project_markdown.article:
«Markdown generation» controls how raw text (after extraction and optional image handling) becomes the final Markdown.

Modes:
- Keep as is — text is unchanged; only written to .md files.
- Process with LLM — chunks are sent to the selected chat model with a system prompt; the model returns formatted Markdown (headings, lists, tables, etc.).

Available providers and models depend on API keys. Add keys in app settings from the main screen.

Chat models are configured under «Model settings» on the main screen. Any text chat model works for this stage (image input is not required).
project_pipeline.threads_section_descrip: The application supports multi-threaded document processing: more threads finish the project faster, but you may hit API rate limits.
project_pipeline.create_documents_index_: When "Yes", index.md is created in result: for each file — a heading with link, description, date, and tags from metadata.
project_pipeline.extract_threads_hint: Parallel jobs for extracting text and structure from files (PDF, office formats, etc.). More threads speed up batches on multi-core CPUs but increase disk and memory load.
project_pipeline.image_processing_thread: Parallelism for the image recognition stage (OCR, vision, preparation for output). Tune to CPU cores and external API limits.
project_pipeline.yandex_ocr_banner:
When using Yandex OCR for images, only one
processing thread applies — RPS limits on the service side.
project_pipeline.markdown_threads_hint: How many documents are processed at once during Markdown generation, including LLM calls when the mode requires it.
project_pipeline.tagging_threads_hint: Concurrent LLM requests in "Batch" mode (Tagging section). Not used for "Linear" or "Skip".
project_pipeline.article:
The General tab: index in result and parallelism per pipeline stage.

Create documents index — when "Yes", index.md is created in result: for each document a block with linked heading, description, date, and tags.

Discovery always runs in a single thread — there is no separate setting.

Data extraction — parallel processing of different source files. Image recognition — parallel processing of images on that stage. Markdown generation — parallel processing on the Markdown output stage. Tagging — maximum concurrent LLM calls in "Batch" mode in the Tagging section (linear vs batch is chosen there).

If Yandex OCR is selected for images, only one thread is effectively used on that stage due to service limits.
project_tagging.mode_desc.skip: No LLM tagging; documents continue without tag changes from this stage.
project_tagging.mode_desc.linear: Documents are processed one by one; new tags from each file (if created) feed into later documents. Requires provider and model.
project_tagging.mode_desc.parallel: Several documents at once (thread count on the General tab, often 4–8): faster, but tag names may vary. Requires provider and model.
project_tagging.field.create_tags_hint: If «No», this stage does not call the LLM: tags and related fields from the model response are not filled.
project_tagging.field.tag_format_hint: How to normalize tags for the model and when writing: Tag_name (default) — first letter of each _-segment upper case, all-caps segments unchanged; or tag_name (all lower). Spaces become underscores.
project_tagging.field.description_hint: If «Yes», the YAML response should include description — short document summary (metadata).
project_tagging.field.date_hint: If «Yes», the YAML response should include date (YYYY-MM-DD or empty) — document date from content.
project_tagging.hint.model: Chat model for extracting tags from markup
project_tagging.hint.temperature: Response randomness (0.0 = deterministic). Not all models support this parameter; if an error occurs, set the value to 1.0.
project_tagging.hint.extra_instructions: System messages add YAML instructions (tags; plus description/date when enabled) and known tags. Add your own guidance here.
project_tagging.hint.start_tags: Comma-separated or one tag per line. Passed to the model when parsing documents.
project_tagging.article:
«Tagging» runs after Markdown generation. Default is «Batch» (provider and model required; otherwise no LLM).

Modes:
- Skip — no LLM; tags stay as after Markdown.
- Linear — one document at a time; new tags propagate to later files.
- Batch — parallel processing (thread count on General, often 4–8); faster, possible synonym tags.

Providers/models depend on API keys (same as Markdown).

Configure chat models under «Model settings»; text models suffice.

Extra instructions: YAML format is fixed by the app; this field adds your preferences.

Initial tags: comma or line-separated — base list for system.

Tag documents — if «No», no LLM on this stage regardless of mode.

Tag format — Tag_name or tag_name; applies to initial set, prompts, and model output.

Description and date — when «Yes», YAML may include description and/or date.
settings.title: Settings
settings.application.article:
Configure the interface language, application debug mode, and system log level.

The interface language is loaded from the app's locale files and stored in the configuration.

Debug mode adds providers for testing and writes more detailed logs; keep it off for normal use.

The log level controls which messages are written to the log file (DEBUG is the most verbose, CRITICAL only critical events).
settings.application.desc.language: GUI language; stored in the application configuration.
settings.application.desc.debug: Adds providers for testing the app and saves detailed logs about its operation.
settings.application.desc.log_level: Minimum level of messages written to the log file.
settings.llm.desc.gateway_timeout: Maximum time to wait for an API response when calling LLM providers.
settings.llm.article:
LLM providers are used in pipeline stages that need text models (for example Markdown generation or tagging).

If a provider offers multimodal models (they accept images), you can use them to recognize text in documents.

In the row labeled with the provider name, choose "Enabled" and enter the API key for each service you use. When "Disabled", key fields are hidden. Keys are stored in the application configuration on this machine.

Gateway timeout is the maximum wait time for an API response (in seconds). Increase it on slow networks; very high values slow down failure detection.
settings.ocr.article:
Yandex OCR is used during image processing when the project uses OCR mode.

In the "Yandex OCR" row, choose "Enabled" and enter Key ID and Key Secret from Yandex Cloud. When "Disabled", the key block is hidden. Without valid keys, Yandex recognition will be unavailable.

Keys are stored locally in app.ini (or the linked configuration store).
settings.save_modal.title: Settings
